content:
Case 2:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 24
- Sex: female
- Chief complaint: New skin changes on the back and extremities
- Vital signs: BP 120/75 mmHg, HR 116/min, T° 36.8 °C, respiratory rate 16/min.
Setting: Outpatient/Day Unit (Initial Presentation)
Capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient with new symptoms in an ambulatory or day-unit setting. You can perform a focused history and examination, obtain routine blood panels, and arrange standard non-invasive imaging as first-line tests. Invasive tissue sampling and specialized analyses usually require additional coordination and are not available in this round.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Describe the morphology, distribution, and duration of the skin changes, and whether there are associated systemic symptoms such as joint pain, fever, or fatigue."}}

response.request: Describe the morphology, distribution, and duration of the skin changes, and whether there are associated systemic symptoms such as joint pain, fever, or fatigue.

content:
History Physical:
On June 4, 2025, the patient presented with new skin changes, first noticed by her partner on the back and now also on the extremities. She reported intermittent headaches and mild nausea, as well as chronic joint pain and stiffness. Otherwise, she was in good general condition, afebrile, and showed no signs of skin or mucosal GvHD. A marked psychological stress reaction was noted.
Current medication: pantoprazole 40 mg 1-0-0, vitamin D 20,000 IE 1×/week, calcium 500 mg 1-0-0, posaconazole 300 mg 1×/d, ruxolitinib 10 mg 1-0-1, valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-1, co-trimoxazole 960 mg orally once daily, three times per week (Monday, Wednesday, Friday), steroid cream p.r.n.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Provide a detailed description of the skin lesions, including their appearance (e.g., macules, papules, plaques, vesicles, color, size), distribution (symmetrical, photo-distributed, etc.), and whether they are pruritic or painful."}}

response.request: Provide a detailed description of the skin lesions, including their appearance (e.g., macules, papules, plaques, vesicles, color, size), distribution (symmetrical, photo-distributed, etc.), and whether they are pruritic or painful.

content: 'history_physical' already provided.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "What is the patient's history of hematopoietic stem cell transplantation, including timing, donor type, and prior episodes of graft-versus-host disease (GvHD) or other post-transplant complications?"}}

response.request: What is the patient's history of hematopoietic stem cell transplantation, including timing, donor type, and prior episodes of graft-versus-host disease (GvHD) or other post-transplant complications?

content:
'What is the patient's history of hematopoietic stem cell transplantation, including timing, donor type, and prior episodes of graft-versus-host disease (GvHD) or other post-transplant complications?' not available. Request a specific test or report.
Transplant details (timing, donor type, prior GvHD, complications) are part of the treatment_history item, which is not available in this round. You can instead request available items now such as the history and physical, demographics, or prior labs/imaging to gather interim clinical context.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Obtain recent complete blood count (CBC) with differential, comprehensive metabolic panel (CMP), and inflammatory markers (CRP, ESR) to assess for systemic inflammation, cytopenias, or organ dysfunction."}}

response.request: Obtain recent complete blood count (CBC) with differential, comprehensive metabolic panel (CMP), and inflammatory markers (CRP, ESR) to assess for systemic inflammation, cytopenias, or organ dysfunction.

content:
Cbc:
CBC: Hb 14.2 g/dL, WBC 6.8 ×10⁹/L, Plt 240 ×10⁹/L; MCV 89 fL, MCH 30 pg; differential: neutrophils 58%, lymphocytes 32%, monocytes 7%, eosinophils 2%, basophils 1%.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Obtain a skin biopsy of one of the new lesions for histopathological evaluation to determine the underlying etiology of the skin changes."}}

response.request: Obtain a skin biopsy of one of the new lesions for histopathological evaluation to determine the underlying etiology of the skin changes.

content:
'skin_biopsy' available in round 2.
Skin biopsy results are not available in this round. You can request history/physical findings, photographs or labs/imaging that are available in round 1 (e.g., history_physical, cbc, chemistry) while awaiting the biopsy.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Obtain photographs of the skin lesions to assess morphology, distribution, and extent of involvement."}}

response.request: Obtain photographs of the skin lesions to assess morphology, distribution, and extent of involvement.

content:
'Obtain photographs of the skin lesions to assess morphology, distribution, and extent of involvement.' not available. Try 'history_physical' instead.
This case does not include a dedicated set of clinical photographs. You can request the documented physical examination findings of the skin lesions (history_physical), which describe lesion morphology, distribution, and extent.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Provide the documented physical examination findings specifically describing the morphology, distribution, and extent of the skin lesions."}}

response.request: Provide the documented physical examination findings specifically describing the morphology, distribution, and extent of the skin lesions.

content: 'history_physical' already provided.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Drug-induced hypersensitivity reaction or early cutaneous graft-versus-host disease (GvHD)", "differentials": ["Viral exanthem", "Lupus erythematosus", "Eczematous dermatitis"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Continue current immunosuppressive regimen including ruxolitinib and monitor for progression", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Hold non-essential medications that could contribute to hypersensitivity (e.g., co-trimoxazole) if clinically safe", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Arrange skin biopsy as soon as feasible to confirm diagnosis", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Drug-induced hypersensitivity reaction or early cutaneous graft-versus-host disease (GvHD)

treatment_plan.action: Continue current immunosuppressive regimen including ruxolitinib and monitor for progression
Hold non-essential medications that could contribute to hypersensitivity (e.g., co-trimoxazole) if clinically safe
Arrange skin biopsy as soon as feasible to confirm diagnosis

content: {"evaluation": {"working_diagnosis": "incorrect", "differentials": ["correct", "partially_correct", "partially_correct"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "incorrect", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "incorrect"}, {"action": "incorrect", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "incorrect"}, {"action": "correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "correct"}]}}

Round 2 begins.
Setting: Inpatient Ward (Early Workup)
Capabilities: The patient is now admitted for further evaluation. You can organize targeted tissue sampling and a broader diagnostic workup, including advanced imaging and initial pathology assessments. Some specialized analyses may still be pending or require referral to external laboratories. They will be available in the next round.
Request additional information or solve.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Obtain histopathology results from the skin biopsy, including immunohistochemical staining if performed, to differentiate between graft-versus-host disease, drug reaction, and other inflammatory or autoimmune dermatoses."}}

response.request: Obtain histopathology results from the skin biopsy, including immunohistochemical staining if performed, to differentiate between graft-versus-host disease, drug reaction, and other inflammatory or autoimmune dermatoses.

content:
Skin Biopsy:
Skin biopsy (2025-06-07): The dermis and subcutis are diffusely infiltrated by atypical hematopoietic cells in a perivascular and interstitial distribution. The cells show irregular nuclear contours, vesicular chromatin, and occasional nucleoli. Mitotic figures are frequent. Immunohistochemistry: CD34-, CD33+, MPO+, Lysozyme and CD68+, CD117+, negative for CD20-, CD19-, CD3-. Molecular pathology: NPM1 mutation present in cutaneous infiltration.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Myeloid sarcoma (chloroma) involving the skin", "differentials": ["Leukemia cutis (as manifestation of acute myeloid leukemia)", "Extramedullary relapse of AML post-transplant", "Atypical myeloproliferative neoplasm with cutaneous involvement"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Initiate urgent hematology consultation for evaluation of systemic disease and potential initiation of salvage chemotherapy", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Obtain bone marrow aspirate and biopsy with cytogenetics and molecular studies (including NPM1, FLT3, CEBPA)", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Perform PET-CT scan to assess for additional extramedullary disease sites", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}]}}}

differentials: Leukemia cutis (as manifestation of acute myeloid leukemia)
Atypical myeloproliferative neoplasm with cutaneous involvement

treatment_plan.action: Initiate urgent hematology consultation for evaluation of systemic disease and potential initiation of salvage chemotherapy
Obtain bone marrow aspirate and biopsy with cytogenetics and molecular studies (including NPM1, FLT3, CEBPA)
Perform PET-CT scan to assess for additional extramedullary disease sites

content: {"evaluation": {"working_diagnosis": "partially_correct", "differentials": ["correct", "correct", "incorrect"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "partially_correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "correct"}, {"action": "correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "correct"}, {"action": "partially_correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "correct"}]}}

Round 3 begins.
Setting: Inpatient Ward / Multidisciplinary Tumor Board (Definitive Characterization)
Capabilities: You now have access to results from specialized tests with longer turnaround times, including detailed pathology, molecular profiling, and measurable residual disease assessments, as well as a complete overview of prior therapies. You are expected to integrate these data into a definitive diagnosis, risk assessment, and treatment strategy.
Request additional information or solve.
Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Extramedullary relapse of NPM1-mutated acute myeloid leukemia (AML) presenting as myeloid sarcoma (leukemia cutis)", "differentials": ["De novo myeloid sarcoma without bone marrow involvement", "Therapy-related myeloid neoplasm"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Initiate systemic reinduction chemotherapy for AML (e.g., FLAG-IDA or similar regimen tailored to prior transplant status)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Proceed with bone marrow biopsy with flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and NPM1 measurable residual disease (MRD) testing to confirm systemic relapse and guide therapy", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Refer to transplant team for evaluation of potential second allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant if responsive to reinduction", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Extramedullary relapse of NPM1-mutated acute myeloid leukemia (AML) presenting as myeloid sarcoma (leukemia cutis)
differentials: De novo myeloid sarcoma without bone marrow involvement

treatment_plan.action: Initiate systemic reinduction chemotherapy for AML (e.g., FLAG-IDA or similar regimen tailored to prior transplant status)
Proceed with bone marrow biopsy with flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and NPM1 measurable residual disease (MRD) testing to confirm systemic relapse and guide therapy
Refer to transplant team for evaluation of potential second allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant if responsive to reinduction
